title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Examples behavior contributes positive environment community include: Demonstrating empathy kindness toward people respectful differing opinions, viewpoints, experiences Giving gracefully accepting constructive feedback Accepting responsibility apologizing affected mistakes, learning experience Focusing best just us individuals, overall community Examples unacceptable behavior include: use sexualized language imagery, sexual attention advances kind Trolling, insulting derogatory comments, personal political attacks Public private harassment Publishing others’ private information, physical email address, without explicit permission conduct reasonably considered inappropriate professional setting

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Code Conduct applies within community spaces, also applies individual officially representing community public spaces. Examples representing community include using official e-mail address, posting via official social media account, acting appointed representative online offline event.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Instances abusive, harassing, otherwise unacceptable behavior may reported community leaders responsible enforcement codeofconduct@posit.co. complaints reviewed investigated promptly fairly. community leaders obligated respect privacy security reporter incident.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Code Conduct adapted Contributor Covenant, version 2.1, available https://www.contributor-covenant.org/version/2/1/code_of_conduct.html. Community Impact Guidelines inspired [Mozilla’s code conduct enforcement ladder][https://github.com/mozilla/inclusion]. answers common questions code conduct, see FAQ https://www.contributor-covenant.org/faq. Translations available https://www.contributor-covenant.org/translations.

title: Contributing to ssdtests
text: want make bigger change, ’s good idea first file issue make sure someone team agrees ’s needed. ’ve found bug, please file issue illustrates bug minimal reprex (also help write unit test, needed).

title: Contributing to ssdtests
text: Fork package clone onto computer. haven’t done , recommend using usethis::create_from_github("", fork = TRUE). Install development dependences devtools::install_dev_deps(), make sure package passes R CMD check running devtools::check(). R CMD check doesn’t pass cleanly, ’s good idea ask help continuing. Create Git branch pull request (PR). recommend using usethis::pr_init("brief-description--change"). Make changes, commit git, create PR running usethis::pr_push(), following prompts browser. title PR briefly describe change. body PR contain Fixes #issue-number. Begin commit merge messages - indicate user facing changes included NEWS.md. use fledge automatically generate NEWS.md file.

title: Contributing to ssdtests
text: New code follow tidyverse style guide. can use styler package apply styles, please don’t restyle code nothing PR. use roxygen2, Markdown syntax, documentation. use testthat unit tests. Contributions test cases included easier accept.

title: Getting help with ssdtests
text: Start making minimal reproducible example using reprex package. haven’t heard used reprex , ’re treat! Seriously, reprex make R-question-asking endeavors easier (pretty insane ROI five ten minutes ’ll take learn ’s ). additional reprex pointers, check Get help! section tidyverse site.

title: Introduction to ssdtests
text: ssdtests holds slow unstable tests ssdtools. contains user-facing functionality ; job exercise ssdtools code paths run reliably quickly part ssdtools’ CRAN-facing test suite.

title: Introduction to ssdtests
text: test belongs rather ssdtools run reliably part ssdtools’ tests. practice means: Exact snapshots parametric bootstrap confidence limits, whose values reproducible across BLAS/LAPACK implementations, exact comparison holds controlled environment. Slow tests make ssdtools’ suite heavy CRAN, fitting every curated dataset every distribution, looping thousands small-sample fits. Numerically unstable fits (example gompertz) whose results drift run run across platforms. ssdtools keeps fast, portable, structural assertions code paths; ssdtests keeps exact-value stress tests. test fast portable belongs ssdtools, .

title: Introduction to ssdtests
text: Tests live tests/testthat/ organised subject, one test-<subject>.R file per distribution function, example test-lnorm.R, test-hc.R, test-plot.R. separate “unstable” file; test lives subject regardless stable . Shared helpers tests/testthat/helpers.R support common patterns: test_dist2() checks distribution’s parameters can recovered data simulated ssd_r<dist>(). expect_snapshot_data() snapshots data frame CSV, rounding numeric columns chosen number significant figures. expect_snapshot_boot_data() asserts structural pboot bounds snapshots bootstrap result.

title: Introduction to ssdtests
text: Two kinds snapshot used. Deterministic snapshots, point estimates tidy tables, run every platform. rounded 4-6 significant figures via digits argument expect_snapshot_data() minor cross-platform maximum-likelihood differences cause failures. Bootstrap confidence-limit snapshots (lcl, ucl, se ssd_hc(ci = TRUE) ssd_hp(ci = TRUE)) reproducible across BLAS/LAPACK implementations. guarded skip_on_ci() placed bootstrap call, expensive, platform-dependent comparison runs locally. bootstrap tests skip_on_ci(), compare machine generated .

title: Introduction to ssdtests
text: Two tests fit full collection curated datasets returned ssddata::ssd_data_sets() snapshot combined table. test-bcanz-hc.R fits BCANZ distributions dataset snapshots model-averaged hazard concentrations default proportions: test-hc5-gm.R fits every valid distribution (ssd_dists_all()) dataset snapshots per-distribution HC5 percentage geometric mean concentrations, full grid NA distribution fails fit: tables rounded precision reproducible across platforms (7 significant figures HC5 table, established cross-platform measurement).
code: library(ssdtools) library(ssddata)  fit <- ssd_fit_bcanz(ssddata::ccme_boron) ssd_hc_bcanz(fit) fit <- ssd_fit_dists(ssddata::ccme_boron, dists = ssd_dists_all()) hc <- ssd_hc(fit, proportion = 0.05, average = FALSE) hc$est / ssddata::gm_mean(ssddata::ccme_boron$Conc) * 100

title: Introduction to ssdtests
text: scripts/ssdtools-coverage.R reports ssdtools line coverage produced ssdtests suite. clones ssdtools branch corresponds current ssdtests repo branch, instruments covr, runs ssdtests tests , prints overall per-file coverage: Run locally rather CI, skip_on_ci() tests coverage comes .

title: Introduction to ssdtests
text: Run suite testthat::test_local() devtools::test(). bootstrap stress tests skip_on_ci() run locally. Snapshots generated locally (macOS); review changes testthat::snapshot_review() accept testthat::snapshot_accept(). See CONTRIBUTING.md contributor-facing version conventions.

title: Tests for ssdtools
text: goal ssdtests hold slow unstable tests ssdtools package. user-facing functionality . test belongs rather ssdtools run reliably quickly part ssdtools’ CRAN-facing suite: exact parametric bootstrap confidence-limit snapshots reproducible across platforms, slow tests fitting every curated dataset every distribution, numerically unstable fits. ssdtools keeps fast, portable, structural assertions code paths. tests organised subject tests/testthat/ (one test-<subject>.R file per distribution function), scripts/ssdtools-coverage.R reports ssdtools coverage suite produces. See vignette("ssdtests") overview CONTRIBUTING.md conventions.

title: ssdtests 0.2.0
text: Added regression tables fit every curated ssddata dataset: model-averaged BCANZ hazard concentrations (test-bcanz-hc) per-distribution HC5 percentage geometric mean data (test-hc5-gm). Added scripts/ssdtools-coverage.R report ssdtools line coverage produced ssdtests suite. Added introductory vignette (vignette("ssdtests")) expanded README. Reorganized tests subject documented snapshot-stability conventions ssdtests-versus-ssdtools placement rule CONTRIBUTING.md. Standardized continuous integration poissonconsulting reusable workflows. Test hygiene: revived removed permanently-skipped tests, removed redundant tests, pruned unused dependencies.

title: ssdtests 0.1.0
text: Initial release version.
